creator: freebsd-2024@virtual-estates.net
text:
Chris Costello once wrote:

> On Tue, Jun 1, 1999, Mikhail Teterin wrote:
> > 	mi@chubby:~ (84) ls -l /dev/tun3
> > 	crw-rw----  1 mi  dialer   52,   3 Jun  1 11:00 /dev/tun3
> > 	mi@chubby:~ (85) groups
> > 	mi dialer operator wheel
> > 	mi@chubby:~ (86) cat /dev/tun3
> > 	cat: /dev/tun3: Operation not permitted
> > 	mi@chubby:~ (87) whoami
> > 	mi
> 
>    No, it  just means you can't  use 'cat' on tunX.  Try running 'ppp'
> with it.

Aha, and  what's so special  about cat(1)? I  can not open(2)  the file,
despite favorable  permissions, that's it.  cat is just an  example. Ppp
works, because it is suid root. Yours,

	-mi
